prerequisites: A Vectara account with access to the Vectara Console (https://console.vectara.com).
Your Vectara customer_id, found by clicking your name in the Vectara Console and opening your account details.
A destination corpus name (corpus_name). If a corpus with that name does not exist, the connector creates it for you.
Within the target corpus, a custom metadata filter attribute named _ab_stream (string typed) must be defined; the connector uses it to identify source streams.
An OAuth 2.0 App Client created in the Console (API access page) so you have a client_id and client_secret.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates using the OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant. Supply the oauth2 object containing the App Client's client_id and client_secret (oauth2.client_secret is the secret value). The connector exchanges these at Vectara's token endpoint for a short-lived JWT access token, which is sent as a Bearer token on API requests. Create the App Client from the Vectara Console API access page (Create app client), then copy its ID and secret.

setupSteps.title: Locate your customer ID
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Vectara Console and click your name to open your account details. Copy your customer ID and use it for the customer_id field.

setupSteps.title: Prepare the destination corpus
setupSteps.body: Choose the corpus_name for the destination. You may point at an existing corpus or let the connector create one if the name is not found. In the corpus, define a string-typed metadata filter attribute named _ab_stream, which the connector requires to identify source streams.

setupSteps.title: Create an OAuth 2.0 App Client
setupSteps.body: In the Console, go to the API access page (App Clients) and click Create app client. Enter a name and description, select the appropriate roles, and click Create. Copy the generated Client ID and Client Secret. These applications use the client credentials grant to generate JWT tokens.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide corpus_name and customer_id, and set the oauth2 object with the client_id and client_secret from your App Client. Optionally set text_fields (source fields indexed as searchable text), title_field (source field used as the document title), metadata_fields (source fields stored as document metadata), and parallelize to enable concurrent ingestion.

sources.title: App Clients for OAuth | Vectara Docs
